mnemonics.title: Projection Matrix Properties
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'P²P': P-squared equals P (idempotent), P-transpose equals P (symmetric for orthogonal). Think: 'Please Promote Yourself' → P = P² and P = Pᵀ.

mnemonics.title: Projection Formula
mnemonics.content: proj_v(u) = (v·u / u·u) u. Mnemonic: 'Dot over Dot' - dot product of vector with direction divided by dot product of direction with itself, multiplied by direction.

mnemonics.title: Least Squares Solution
mnemonics.content: x = (AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀb. Mnemonic: 'A-Inverse A-Inverse Transpose B' or think of it as 'A-plus b' where A-plus = (AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀ.

acronyms.usage: Dimensionality reduction technique using orthogonal projections onto principal components (eigenvectors of covariance matrix).
Decomposition A = UΣVᵀ used to compute pseudoinverse and projections; singular values relate to projection lengths.
Sparse coding algorithm using orthogonal projections onto atom dictionaries.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine shining a flashlight perpendicular to a line/plane - the shadow cast is the orthogonal projection. The original vector and its projection form a right triangle with the error vector.
Think of I-P as a mirror that reflects across the subspace W. The projection P keeps you on W while I-P sends you to the orthogonal complement.
Vectors in the subspace (range) stay fixed (eigenvalue = 1); vectors perpendicular (null space) become zero (eigenvalue = 0).

analogies.analogy: Just as your shadow on a wall is the 'projection' of your body onto a 2D plane, a vector's projection onto a subspace is its 'shadow' in that lower-dimensional space.
Fitting a line through scattered points is like trying to project the data points onto the space of all possible lines - the projection gives the 'closest' line.
A projection matrix acts like a filter that lets through only the component in a certain direction while blocking the rest (sending it to zero).
